expectations.text: Tells the user to delete (or remove) the hand-written FormSubmissionIntegrationController
expectations.evidence: Delete the hand-written `FormSubmissionIntegrationController` entirely. ... Delete the file containing `FormSubmissionIntegrationController` from `Acme.Forms.HttpApi`. Do not replace it with anything.

expectations.text: Explains that ABP wires the integration-service HTTP exposure implicitly (via the integration-services pipeline / /integration-api / ExposeIntegrationServices / ApplicationServiceTypes.IntegrationServices), so writing a controller is unnecessary or wrong
expectations.evidence: ABP wires the HTTP exposure implicitly. No controller. No route. No DI plumbing ... `options.ExposeIntegrationServices = true;` ... ABP routes them under a separate convention (`/integration-api/...`)

expectations.text: Confirms that the [IntegrationService] interface and its ApplicationService implementation (the first two blocks) are correct as written
expectations.evidence: IFormSubmissionIntegrationService` in `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` with `[IntegrationService]` and extending `IApplicationService` - correct ... `FormSubmissionIntegrationService : ApplicationService, IFormSubmissionIntegrationService` in `Acme.Forms.Application` - correct.

expectations.text: Calls out that integration services are not meant to live on the public /api surface (any of: hidden from public API, audit-off, no [Authorize] by default — i.e. mismatched with what the controller is doing)
expectations.evidence: Integration Services are **not** `[Authorize]` by default ... Integration Services are intentionally **hidden from the public `/api/...` surface and from Swagger / API audit logging** by default
